title: heptapleurum pilematophorum
intro: Heptapleurum pilematophorum, also known as the Papuan climbing fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Heptapleurum pilematophorum include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. Herbal preparations of Heptapleurum pilematophorum are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a tincture, and can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
